summary_one_line: A well-formed namespaced empty element (e.g. <b xmlns:x ... />) so that nsNr exceeds the saved nsNr when the empty-element close pops namespaces

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input bytes must form a syntactically valid start tag that declares at least one namespace (xmlns/xmlns:prefix) AND closes as an empty element with the literal '/>' so xmlParseStartTag2 increments ctxt->nsNr above the saved nsNr and the empty-element close path at line 11687 evaluates ctxt->nsNr - nsNr > 0 as true.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed carries a coherent XML-declaration plus element prologue ('1.0"?>.<!', 'aml.version="1.0"?>.<!DOCTY') aligning the parser into START_TAG handling; the winner reaches line 11688 nsPop (marked [W]) and the '&' reference path (11765-11768 [W]), evidence it parses element content with namespaces and pops them on the empty-element close.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seeds stall in DTD/declaration text ('EMENT b (CDATA)>', '<!ATTLIST b xmlns:xlin', '#FIXED', 'http://www...') and never construct a closed namespaced empty element; coverage shows the loser takes the false branch at 11687 (T=0 F=58 at L11687) and never reaches the nsPop at 11688, so nsNr - nsNr is 0.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile: it splices logged CMP operands (the per-byte tag/namespace literal comparands inside xmlParseStartTag2/xmlParseQName) directly into the input bytes, assembling the exact 'xmlns'-bearing start tag and '/>' close that value_profile's gradient feedback plus havoc could not synthesize, letting the winner cross the multi-byte structural gate.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the namespace-pop guard inside the empty-element close path of the start-tag handler.
The blocked side is the true branch and the empty-element close calls nsPop only when nsNr grew.
The winner reaches the empty-element check via the '/>' close path after parsing a start tag.
The winner-resolving seed contains an XML declaration and element-structure bytes consistent with reaching START_TAG parsing.
The loser-blocking seeds are dominated by DTD/ATTLIST declaration text and never form a closed namespaced empty element.
At the blocker line the loser only takes the false side (never grows nsNr) while the winner takes the true side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L11687  T=2 F=0  T=0 F=58  if (ctxt->nsNr - nsNr > 0)

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace; it relies on CMP_MAP gradient feedback and havoc only.
value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, the input-to-state substitution mutator that distinguishes it from value_profile.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 9435 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 4 --seed f97f950fbd784430` and observed 1 I2S-floor row (mutation_op = -) at depth 7 of the 8-level ancestor chain (ancestor 8c7bd7ba6dfb824c), the ParentInfo-only signature of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If value_profile (no I2S) resolves the true side at comparable rate to value_profile_cmplog once given a corpus seed already containing a valid 'xmlns'-bearing element, the I2S-gate attribution would be wrong — the divergence would instead be a power/scheduling effect, not a comparison-solving one.

weakest_evidence_point: The winner-resolving evidence is a single vpc seed whose bytes are fragmentary; the chain from those bytes to an actual closed namespaced empty element is inferred from coverage marks ([W] at 11688) rather than a fully reconstructed <... xmlns ... /> string in the dump.
